title: MP donates pails and chairs
author: Mussa Kabichi
body: The Malawi Institute of Education (MIE) has initiated a pre-pilot phase of a new pre-primary curriculum in Salima District, Nsanje District, and Dedza District, in a move aimed at strengthening early childhood education. The exercise is being conducted in selected government primary schools with pre-primary sections, involving about 15 schools in each district. The program is being supported by UNICEF, which has financed implementation activities and provided learning materials. MIE officials say teachers in the participating schools were oriented ahead of the rollout to ensure effective delivery of the curriculum. The new framework focuses on Creative Arts, Chichewa, Numeracy, and English, with emphasis on oral language development and early literacy. According to MIE, the pre-pilot is intended to assess and refine the curriculum before wider implementation. A national pilot is scheduled to begin in September and will run for one year, with support from the Global Partnership for Education through the World Bank. All teaching and learning materials currently being used remain in draft form and are not yet available for public use, with insights from the exercise expected to guide the finalisation of the curriculum before nationwide rollout.

title: Gvt urged to raise bridge
author: Mussa Kabichi
body: Fly P Foundation visited Nkhorongo Primary School, where it donated exercise books, ball pens, and pencils to 40 learners from vulnerable families.  The initiative aims to support underprivileged students and enhance their access to essential learning materials.
